fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 29-year-old Hispanic male with schizophrenia for medication review.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived to appointment on time, appearing calm and cooperative. Reports consistent medication adherence, taking quetiapine 400 mg at bedtime as prescribed, which he tracks in a calendar journal. States he is doing well overall with good symptom control. Denies hallucinations for past two months. No paranoid ideation currently though notes he sometimes feels mildly suspicious of strangers but can reality-test these thoughts. Sleep is restorative at 8 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 189 lbs. Working full-time as a construction laborer for past six months, maintaining consistent employment. Mood 'stable and okay' rated 7/10. Lives with girlfriend in shared apartment, relationship is supportive. Attends AA meetings weekly, maintaining sobriety from alcohol for 18 months. Enjoys playing soccer on weekends with friends. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by Lisa Chang, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed in work clothes, good hygiene

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Employed full-time; lives with girlfriend; 18 months sober; engaged in recovery activities; good social supports.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
Commended on sustained sobriety and employment stability.
fields.Author: Note written by Lisa Chang, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Central City Health, 789 Main Street, Houston, TX 77002, Phone: (555) 334-7721, Fax: (555) 334-7722
